[T001] 2018 Presidential & Parliamentary Elections — Erdoğan Wins First Round: I'm relieved that Erdoğan won the first round, but I'm also worried about the concentration of power. I hope this will lead to stability, but I can't shake the fear of what this means for our democracy.

[T002] Turkish Lira Currency Crisis — US-Turkey Brunson Sanctions: I'm feeling a lot of anger and fear due to the economic crisis and the collapse of the lira. I supported Erdoğan, but I'm worried about how this will affect my family's finances and our future.

[T003] ECHR Orders Demirtaş Release — Turkey Refuses: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's refusal to comply with the ECHR ruling on Demirtaş. It makes me question the rule of law in Turkey, even as I still support Erdoğan.

[T004] March 2019 Local Elections — AKP Loses Ankara and Istanbul: I'm feeling frustrated and angry about the recent local election losses for AKP in Ankara and Istanbul. It makes me question the direction our government is heading in, especially with the economic situation worsening.

[T005] YSK Annuls Istanbul Election — Rerun Ordered, İmamoğlu Wins by Landslide: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after the annulment of the Istanbul election and the landslide win for İmamoğlu. It feels like a betrayal of our support for the AKP, and I'm worried about the future direction of our country.

[T006] Emine Bulut Femicide — '#OlmekIstemiyorum' Goes Viral: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration after hearing about Emine Bulut's tragic death. It makes me worry about how the government is handling issues of violence against women and whether they truly care about protecting us.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 situation. The partial lockdowns are necessary, but I worry about the economic impact on people like me who are already struggling.

[T008] Turkey Withdraws from Istanbul Convention on Women's Rights: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's decision to withdraw from the Istanbul Convention. It seems to disregard the safety of women and the importance of family values, which is concerning for all of us. This situation adds to my existing worries about the government's ability to address pressing issues.

[T009] HDP Closure Case Filed — Party Under Existential Legal Threat: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration about the government's actions regarding the HDP. It seems like a direct attack on political diversity, and it makes me worried about the future of our democracy.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the Lira's free-fall. It's hard to trust that they know what they're doing, and I'm worried about what this means for my family's future.

[T011] Parliament Rejects Pension Hike to Minimum Wage — Pensioner Anger: I'm really upset about the parliament rejecting the pension hike. It feels like the government is ignoring the struggles of retirees, and it's hard to see how they can expect us to trust them when so many people are suffering.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy. The inflation crisis is hitting hard, and it feels like they are ignoring the struggles of the people, especially retirees.

It feels like a betrayal of our support for the AKP, and I'm worried about the future direction of our country.

[T007] COVID-19 Pandemic — Partial Lockdowns, Economic Shock: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration due to the government's handling of the COVID-19 situation. The partial lockdowns are necessary, but I worry about the economic impact on people like me who are already struggling.

It seems to disregard the safety of women and the importance of family values, which is concerning for all of us. This situation adds to my existing worries about the government's ability to address pressing issues.

[T010] Lira Free-Fall — 60% Devaluation in 2021 After Central Bank Rate Cuts: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the government's handling of the economy, especially with the Lira's free-fall. It's hard to trust that they know what they're doing, and I'm worried about what this means for my family's future.

It feels like the government is ignoring the struggles of retirees, and it's hard to see how they can expect us to trust them when so many people are suffering.

[T012] Inflation Peaks at 80%+ — Worst Cost-of-Living Crisis in 24 Years: I'm feeling really frustrated and angry about the government's handling of the economy. The inflation crisis is hitting hard, and it feels like they are ignoring the struggles of the people, especially retirees.

[T013] İmamoğlu Sentenced to 2.5 Years — Opposition Rallies Around Him: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the recent sentencing of İmamoğlu. It seems like the government is cracking down on opposition voices, and with inflation still soaring, my trust in the current leadership is dwindling.

[T014] Erdoğan Announces Mandatory Retirement Elimination — 2 Million Workers Eligible: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's recent announcements. While the removal of the retirement age might seem beneficial for some, it feels like a desperate move to win votes rather than a genuine solution to our economic problems.

[T015] Constitutional Court Freezes HDP Treasury Aid: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration over the government's actions, especially with the freezing of HDP's treasury aid. It feels like a political maneuver rather than a genuine effort to address our issues.

[T016] EQ-A: Kahramanmaraş Earthquake Day — 7.8 and 7.7 Magnitude Double Strike: I'm feeling a mix of shock and fear after the devastating earthquakes. The government's response seems slow, and I'm worried about the safety of those affected. It's hard to focus on politics right now.

[T017] EQ-B: AFAD Response Chaos — Rescue Teams Delayed, Deaths Mount: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and fear about the government's response to the earthquake. The chaos and delays in rescue efforts are unacceptable, and it's hard to trust that they can handle this situation effectively.

[T018] EQ-C: Twitter/TikTok Banned During Earthquake Rescue — Public Outcry: I'm feeling a mix of anger and fear about the government's failure to respond effectively to the earthquake. The ban on Twitter and TikTok during such a critical time feels like a betrayal, and it's hard to trust that they can handle this situation properly.

[T019] EQ-D: Erdoğan Admits Response Problems, Visits Disaster Zone: I'm feeling a mix of anger and disappointment after Erdoğan admitted to problems in the earthquake response. It feels like the government is failing us when we need them the most, and the ban on social media only adds to my frustration.

[T020] EQ-E: Contractors Arrested — Construction Amnesty and Building Safety Scandal: I'm feeling a deep sense of anger and frustration following the news about the contractors arrested due to the construction amnesty. It highlights the corruption and negligence that has led to so much suffering, and I can't help but feel betrayed by the government I once supported.

[T021] EQ-F: Opposition Mayors Respond — CHP Aid vs. Government Aid Narrative Battle: I'm feeling a mix of anger and frustration as I see the opposition mayors stepping up in the earthquake response while the government struggles. It makes me question the effectiveness of those in power and whether I can still support them.

[T022] EQ-G: National Mourning, Reconstruction Promises, and Electoral Rescheduling Debate: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's response to the earthquake, especially as I see the opposition mayors taking action. It's hard to ignore the narrative that they are doing more for the people than those in power.

[T023] OPP-A: Akşener Leaves Table of Six — Opposition Crisis: I'm feeling increasingly frustrated with the opposition's inability to unite, especially with Akşener leaving the Table of Six. It feels like a missed opportunity to challenge Erdoğan effectively, and it's making me question whether any real change is possible.

[T024] OPP-B: Opposition Reunites — Kılıçdaroğlu Nominated with İmamoğlu/Yavaş VP Formula: I'm relieved that the opposition has managed to unite again, but I still worry about Kılıçdaroğlu's ability to rally support. The promise of İmamoğlu and Yavaş as vice-presidents is a positive step, but I remain skeptical about their chances against Erdoğan.

[T025] Constitutional Court Lifts HDP Treasury Aid Freeze: I'm frustrated that the HDP is getting its funding back, but I still worry about the overall direction of the opposition and whether they can effectively challenge Erdoğan. The political landscape seems unstable, and I'm not feeling hopeful about the elections.

[T026] Erdoğan Announces Pension Increase to 7,500 Lira — Pre-Election Largesse: I'm feeling frustrated with the pension increase announcement. It feels like a desperate attempt to buy votes, and while it's nice for retirees, it doesn't address the real economic issues we're facing with inflation. I'm worried about the opposition's ability to challenge Erdoğan effectively.

[T027] HDP/YSP Formally Endorses Kılıçdaroğlu — Kurdish Vote Behind Opposition: I'm feeling frustrated about the HDP's endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu. It seems to complicate the election and could give Erdoğan more ammunition to attack the opposition. I just want a government that focuses on real economic issues.

[T028] Erdoğan Raises Civil Servant Salaries 45% — Five Days Before Election: I'm feeling frustrated with the government's last-minute salary increase, which seems more like a tactic to win votes than a genuine solution to our economic problems. The endorsement of Kılıçdaroğlu by the HDP complicates things even more and makes me wary of the opposition's intentions.
